 Comments   
Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 21/May/26 EDT ]

it seems we are not receiving any file for no touch and there are only 3 old files in the db side.

 

 

We have checked the configuration side and there are only old files in the hue.

It uses header file to generate no touch claims. latest no touch and cdf data will be joining and generating no touch.

So, when joing the data, dup records are present which is causing the issue.




[IT-3072799] MOLHC - Need to identify SNF/IRF data cor CCV Created: 21/May/26 EDT  Updated: 21/May/26 EDT

Status: In Progress (HD1)
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Rajasekaran, Babita Assignee: Devatha, Satish
Resolution: Unresolved Votes: 0
Labels: CCV
Remaining Estimate: 0 minutes
Time Spent: 3 hours
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Date Need By: 22/May/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

I am working on CCV Molina (MOLHC) SNF and IRF expansion. We want to ensure that we are getting SNF/IRF claims in raw data before we reach out to the client. This is the logic to identify SNF and IRF claims:

SNF PDPM Logic:

  • Bill Type 021%
  • HCPCS code starts with B, C, D, E, F, G, I, J, K, M, N or O *
  • HCPCS code has a 2nd character as B, C,D, E,F,G,H, I, J, K,or L
  • HCPCS code has a 3rd character as A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, L, R, S, T, V or W
  • HCPCS code has a 4th character as A, B, C or D

SNF Per Diem logic looks for:

  • Revenue Code: 0191,0192,0193 or 0194 (with or without leading zero)

IRF PPS logic looks for:

  • HCPCS code starts with A, B, C or D (CPT code in Claim Lines)
  • HCPCS codes last four characters are numeric between 0101 and 5104 or 9999

IRF Per Diem logic looks for:

  • Revenue Code is in 0024, 0118, 0128, 0138, 0148 or 0158 (with or without leading zero) (rev_code in claim lines)

 Based on these criteria, can Nile support team confirm if we are getting SNF/IRF data? if possible, can we get a raw data file for validation?

I have a meeting with client Next week Tuesday, if I can get this information prior to that so I can prepare to talk to them.



 Comments   
Comment by Devatha, Satish [ 21/May/26 EDT ]

Shared raw file schema, query results and sample raw file




HCSC Backfill load (IT-2941165)

Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Resolution: Resolved Votes: 0
Labels: CCV

GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Close Notes: Kaiser ARK is still with onboarding team It's not transitioned to Production team yet.
we will let inform onboarding team to take care of this.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Actual Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Kaiser CDF builder failed due to a discrepancy in the schema.

In Kaiserdataarkstaging.dbo.claimsheader table [ClaimFormInd] field has length 1.

In KaiserDataClaimHeader.dbo.CnlyClaimsHeader table [ClaimFormInd] field has length 2.

But in raw data from Nile, we’re receiving 4 characters for data in this field, which is causing this to fail. As per Nightwatchers, Nile team needs to look at claim_form_type_code field as this maps to [ClaimFormInd].



 Comments   
Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 21/May/26 EDT ]

Hi Shrestha, Sajjan , Kaiser ARK is still with onboarding team It's not transitioned to Production team yet.

we will let inform onboarding team to take care of this.

Thanks.

 Description   

Hi Team,
Could you please copy the below mentioned files from the Nile PROD location to the Nile QC location?
for the last week files, 05/11/2026 to latest.

This request is critical as we have early communicated with prod support team.

Nile PROD RAW PATH (Source): /dataingestionprod/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional
Nile Dev RAW PATH (Target): /dataingestionqc/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional

Once copied to QC folder, the owner should be set to "svcpnilediqcraw" and group should be set to "qcunrestricted" and permissions should be 75

 Comments   
Comment by Birney, Kevin [ 18/May/26 EDT ]

I ran the following commands and verified the data matches.  Closing this ticket.

hdfs dfs -du /dataingestionprod/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional

hadoop distcp /dataingestionprod/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional/20260513 /dataingestionqc/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional/
hadoop distcp /dataingestionprod/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional/20260514 /dataingestionqc/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional/

hadoop distcp /dataingestionprod/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional/20260516 /dataingestionqc/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional/

hdfs dfs -chown -R svcpnilediqcraw /dataingestionqc/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional
hdfs dfs -chgrp -R qcunrestricted /dataingestionqc/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional
hdfs dfs -chmod -R 750 /dataingestionqc/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional

hdfs dfs -du /dataingestionprod/raw/unrestricted/5-aetna-traditional/ | grep 20260511

Premera received an inquiry from Cotiviti regarding the following files that were sent on 5/15/26. Looks like the history files are for the Shared Admin line of business. Could I get additional information about the cause of the “excessive line failures”?

prebc_history_institutional_incoming_20260514_1.txt.pgp

prebc_history_professional_incoming_20260514_1.txt.pgp

 Comments   
Comment by Easley, Keaton [ 18/May/26 EDT ]

Spinelli, Adam 

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

prebc_history_professional_incoming_20260514_1.txt.pgp

prebc_history_incoming_1.05-15-2026_1910

PayerShort: PBCSA
Error parsing field CLAIM_BILLED_AMOUNT with format Double occurs 1504 times

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

prebc_history_institutional_incoming_20260514_1.txt.pgp

prebc_ophistory_incoming_1.05-15-2026_1910

PayerShort: PBCSA
Error parsing field VALUE_AMOUNT_1 (VALUE_AMOUNT_01) with format Double occurs 19 times
Error parsing field SUB_NONCOVERED_AMOUNT with format Double occurs 136 times
Error parsing field CLAIM_BILLED_AMOUNT with format Double occurs 196 times

Comment by Spinelli, Adam [ 20/May/26 EDT ]

Hi Keaton,

Please see the below feedback from Premera:

Can you map the below fields to the field names in the GDF format for us? The fields below don’t map our documentation. An example from the file would also help us troubleshoot.

CLAIM_BILLED_AMOUNT

VALUE_AMOUNT_1 (VALUE_AMOUNT_01)

SUB_NONCOVERED_AMOUNT

Comment by Easley, Keaton [ 20/May/26 EDT ]

Supplied mappings and some examples to Adam

Comment by Devatha, Satish [ 20/May/26 EDT ]

Step 1

Updated the below parameters

LoadDate=20260520
Min_Paid_Date=2026-04-01
Max_Paid_Date=2026-04-30

Script path in Bitbucket : RCA_Workflows/UHC-CT/Refine & Denorm Workflow/

updated the Properties file.
Refine_And_Denorm_UHC_Data.properties

Step 2

And ran the below script
path : /home/svcdatameersqoop/workflow/refine_and_denorm/UHC-CT
Script :

oozie job -oozie https://usapardhdpm04.cotiviti.com:11101/oozie/ -config /home/svcdatameersqoop/workflow/refine_and_denorm/UHC-CT/Refine_And_Denorm_UHC_Data.properties -run

Step 3

Received email
Currently job is running

job: 4870861-260504042830459-oozie-oozi-W

Comment by Devatha, Satish [ 21/May/26 EDT ]

Step 4

Job Completed

QA checks are done.

 Description   

Hi Team,

We need data copy for UHC UNET ASO client from Prod to Dev.

Once copied to dev the owner should be set to ‘svcpniledidevraw' and group should be set to 'devunrestricted’ and permission should be 750.

Below are the list of files needed to copy from Prod to Dev -

NILE PROD RAW PATH (SOURCE): /dataingestionprod/raw/unrestricted/23-uhc-unetaso

NILE DEV RAW PATH (TARGET): /dataingestiondev/raw/unrestricted/23-uhc-unetaso

please copy the below 3 files from 2024 to till date

UNET_MBR_BEH_OUT_JAN25.txt.gz.pgp - Cadence of file 9,19,28TH of every month

UNET_MBR_MED_OUT_JAN25.txt.gz.pgp  - Cadence of file 9,19,28TH of every month
unet_mbr_uhgee_out_apr25.txt.gz - Cadence of file - 20th of every month

[IT-3059837] Update " SpecialtyPharmacyProviders" Table in MCL Created: 15/May/26 EDT  Updated: 20/May/26 EDT  Resolved: 20/May/26 EDT

Status: Closed
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Bhegade, Shweta Assignee: Kamsetty, Sravani
Resolution: Resolved Votes: 0
Labels: rca_actian
Remaining Estimate: 0 minutes
Time Spent: 40 minutes
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Attachments: SpecialtyPharmacyProviders.xlsx    
Date Need By: 15/May/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Sub State: Awaiting User Information
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Close Notes: file spooled to hdfs and processed successfully to target table.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Actual Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Please create MCL table " SpecialtyPharmacyProviders " using the attached Excel table. Updates to be provided by ERT (full replace monthly).

  • Who will be the user of this data?
    • This table supports our next gen solution SpotCheck – We will need this table to refine data output for NPI Pharmacy Providers.
  • Can you give a quick description of the table (what this table is about)?
    • The values within the table depict NPI of pharmacy providers, along with their Provider Organization Name (Legal Business Name), Provider Business Practice Location Address City Name and Provider Business Practice Location Address State Name.
  • Would you share with us what the background is for this request (e.g. How are these tables going to be used the users, reports, processes, etc)?
    • This table will be used to support next gen content for SpotCheck. It will be a central location to house all Specialty Pharmacy Providers.
  • How would this data be provided to the Nile Production Support team going forward?
    • We will set up a monthly cadence with ERT to have them review the policy for any changes and deliver updates. (Replace)
  • Will the files be provided to us or should we access/retrieve the data ourselves?
    • Provided by ERT
  • If the files will be given for us, will the format be consistent from delivery to delivery?
    • Yes
  • What will be the frequency/cadence of these files?
    • Monthly

 Comments   
Comment by Bhegade, Shweta [ 15/May/26 EDT ]

SpecialtyPharmacyProviders.xlsx (4.74 MB)

Comment by Deynarian, Michael [ 15/May/26 EDT ]

Hello Kamsetty, Sravani .

This table and pipeline already exist.

Table: [GlobalHCCnlyMedicalCodesLibrary].[dbo].[SpecialtyPharmacyProviders]

Pipeline: dm-mcl-mcl-actian_migration_specialty_pharmacy_provider

Source file location: Y:\Ftp\B2B\MCL-NILE\SpecialtyPharmacyProviders\2026-05-01\SpecialtyPharmacyProviders.xlsx

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Thanks.

Mike

Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 18/May/26 EDT ]

Hi Deynarian, Michael , File has been loaded to target table. Could you please validate.

Awaiting approval to close the ticket.

Comment by Deynarian, Michael [ 19/May/26 EDT ]

I have reviewed the table and it looks good.

Thanks.

Mike

[IT-3058729] P3 Health Partners PPM - Review Handover for Professional Pipeline Reimplementation Created: 14/May/26 EDT  Updated: 25/May/26 EDT  Resolved: 25/May/26 EDT

Status: Closed
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Cheung, Rachael Assignee: Jani, Shaik
Resolution: Resolved Votes: 0
Labels: None
Remaining Estimate: 0 minutes
Time Spent: 1 hour, 30 minutes
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Date Need By: 21/May/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Sub State: Awaiting Release/Deployment
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Close Notes: Request actioned.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Actual Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

As per DIP-38567, we have completed the implementation of a custom CDF for TBA/History Professional files for P3 Health Partners PPM (p3hpi), and would like to hand over this new process to Production Support. 

Here is the change:

  • For TBA/history professional files, appended custom CDF field (#514) PAYER_ORGANIZATION_NAME, mapped from GDF field CH_PAYER_ORGANIZATION_NAME

The new pipelines use the same "optimized" PPM template as the Humana and Wellmark PPM pipelines, currently supported by Production Support. This change is to be applied following the ICMS May release on 5/24, so we have published the new pipelines and enabled EDA starting on 5/24. The file schedule and escalation procedures will remain the same.

Following are the new pipelines in QC and Prod, EDA is enabled starting 5/24:

2026-05-04-68732485 (V1) ppm-p3healthpartners-ezcap-history-professional-claims_GDF2_8_customCDF2_8_optimized

2026-05-04-68052465 (V1) ppm-p3healthpartners-ezcap-tba-professional-claims_GDF2_8_customCDF2_8_optimized

Request: Support these new pipelines starting 5/24 files, and disable old history professional /TBA professional pipelines after 5/23 files are processed.

 Comments   
Comment by Jani, Shaik [ 18/May/26 EDT ]

Since the last file is on 23rd May.

Will set up schedules to the pipeline to avoid conflict.

Status: In Progress (HD1)
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None

Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

We are expanding CareSource and adding TriCare plan.  We have a new Letter Plan Code mapping that needs to be incorporated into the CDF logic.

If the payer short is CSTRI and the INSURANCE_LOB is CTSC, update the LETTER_PLAN_CODE field to CSTR01.

This is also shown on the Crosswalk tab in the "CARHP - TBA_v2.3.1_to_GDF_v2.13_mapping_v3.xlsx" document.  I can forward the document when ready for coding.

 

If the payer short is CSTRI and the INSURANCE_LOB is CTSC, update the LETTER_PLAN_CODE field to CSTR01.

This is also shown on the Crosswalk tab in the "CARHP - TBA_v2.3.1_to_GDF_v2.13_mapping_v3.xlsx" document.  I can forward the document when ready for coding.

 Description   

Can you provide the join logic used for the raw provider files for BCSSC as well as the provider address files mentioned above?  The CSM is asking on behalf of their client.  They do not believe we are using the latest provider addresses they are sending to us. 

Client request to review RAW data files to verify where we pull CH_Billing Provider. From what I have been told, we pull it from the file names below, and my question is what is the RAW data file field we pull from? bcscp_history_institutional_incoming_20260327_1.txt.pgp bcssc_postcheck_medical_incoming_20260321_1091353.txt.pgp Second question is that I see these files on the T1 T2 logs and I'm wondering what is in the files and what are we using it for: bcssc_provideraddress_outgoing_20260327_1.txt.pgp bcssc_provideraddress_incoming_20260327_1.txt.pgp

This is for CCV but the TBA file we ingest comes through PPM so I do not have any insight into their raw provider file or where I can find it.

Can you provide the join logic used for the raw provider files for BCSSC as well as the provider address files mentioned above?  The CSM is asking on behalf of their client.  They do not believe we are using the latest provider addresses they are sending to us.

payer short: Field that I will be using CH_PAYER_SHORT
claim dates : Field that I will be using CH_CLAIM_PAID_DATE

Hi. The claim dates should be Jan 2025 to June 2025. Thanks.

  • Insurance ID is MA_DUAL which will map to GroupNum
  • LOB is DUAL which will map as MCR to ClientDiv

  • CnlyLag is 30 days from paid date

Hence, closing the ticket.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: MEDIUM 4 – 8 hrs
Actual Size: MEDIUM 4 – 8 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

This daily GDF file is created using the daily Kaiser file : OVERLAP_YYYYMMDD

RCA Change Requested: For GDF field ‘EX_Client_Claim_ID’ current logic is excluding claims with Audit Type in ('TPL','Workers Compensation'); please remove this exclusion and let all claims into the GDF file for RCA. Only logic remaining should be a 1x1 mapping.

New Logic: EX_CLIENT_CLAIM_ID = Dataset_Claim Number

This is a client request and needs to be completed as quickly as possible.



 Comments   
Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 14/May/26 EDT ]

Hi, Looking in to it. Working on implementing the changes in the pipeline.

Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 18/May/26 EDT ]

This is Reg kaiser postpay exclusion file change request: https://jiraet.cotiviti.com/browse/IT-3050987

As per the current configurations in the nile, we see nile is not excluding claims with Audit Type in ('TPL','Workers Compensation'). I see we are generating all the claims without any filters.

 

Please let me know if I am missing anything.

Thanks.

Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 20/May/26 EDT ]

We can see that No data dropped from raw to processed.

Comment by Kamsetty, Sravani [ 20/May/26 EDT ]

Closing the ticket, Please open a new ticket if needed. 

Thanks.

dm-centene-healthnet-actian_migration_HNT_provider

dm-uhc-cirrus-actian_migration_entity_cir_cladeny

rccv-bsw-hrp-history_institutional_claims_processed_GDF2_9_CDF2_9
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Actual Size: SMALL 1 – 4 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Multiple client pipelines are failing at the shell-executor stage across multiple instances.

After analyzing the data files and pipeline configurations, we performed force runs for the affected pipelines, and the processes completed successfully.

Further analysis indicates that the failures are occurring specifically at the shell-executor stage, while the underlying data files and configurations appear to be valid.

Action Required:

  • Perform proper analysis for each failed pipeline instance.
  • Manually rerun the impacted pipelines after validation.
  • Monitor subsequent executions to identify any recurring shell-executor stage failures.

 Comments

 Description   

CareSource is adding a new market (FL TriCare) to their data feed. Below are the details:

  • Payer short CSTRI, which will map to ClientLOB
  • Insurance ID is CSTC which will map to GroupNum
  • LOB is Federal which will map as FED to ClientDiv
  • Test file received (see attached image)
  • CnlyExp is 24 months from paid date
  • CnlyLap is 30 days from paid date

Need test file made available in Hue for Ops sign off and validation the above has been put into place.

Once sign off is received, the client will send a backsweep file back to 1/1/2026

Close Notes: Follow-up: Cloudera support case opened to assist with Ranger Admin/log errors. POC Wasim Mohd, Ravisankar Kantimahanti, VenkataAbyas Korada.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: MEDIUM 4 – 8 hrs
Actual Size: MEDIUM 4 – 8 hrs
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Nile production pipeline failed due to intermittent permission denied/Hive exceptions. Investigation focused on Apache Ranger policies and Ranger Admin service instability - Ranger Admin logs showed errors including inability to create files and a database exception.

 Comments   
Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 11/May/26 EDT ]

Service was restored by reattempting the failed pipelines until they completed. Access was restored by creating/updating an Apache Ranger policy to grant required permissions. Jobs began succeeding after retries and policy adjustment.

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 11/May/26 EDT ]

Follow-up: Cloudera support case opened to assist with Ranger Admin/log errors. POC Wasim Mohd, Ravisankar Kantimahanti, VenkataAbyas Korada.




HCSC Backfill load (IT-2941165)

Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 11/May/26 EDT ]

The Third set of files is not being spooled, and we’re facing several challenges. For example, the file name pattern does not include any suffix, and the files have the same name and size as those previously processed. When attempting to spool them, the system indicates that the files have already been processed.

I also tried placing all folders in the HDFS location, but the spooler got stuck, so I reached out to the platform team. As an alternative, we copied the files from FTP to HDFS with Satish’s help. The data is currently still loading.

I have reviewed the files process and get the confirmations from the user. few files data not loaded into the respective tables.

 Description   

Hi Team,

We need data copy for UHC UNET FI client from Prod to Dev.

Once copied to dev the owner should be set to ‘svcpniledidevraw' and group should be set to 'devunrestricted’ and permission should be 750.

Below are the list of files needed to copy from Prod to Dev -

NILE PROD RAW PATH (SOURCE): /dataingestionprod/raw/unrestricted/303-uhc-unet/

NILE DEV RAW PATH (TARGET): /dataingestiondev/raw/unrestricted/24-uhc-unetfi

[IT-3046420] NAS spoolers are getting failed. Created: 10/May/26 EDT  Updated: 12/May/26 EDT  Resolved: 12/May/26 EDT

Status: Closed
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Incident Priority: Priority 1
Reporter: Acharya, Suraj Assignee: Jani, Shaik
Resolution: Closed Votes: 0
Labels: None
Remaining Estimate: 0 minutes
Time Spent: 5 minutes
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Issue Links:
Problem/Incident
is caused by IT-3046419 NAS spoolers are getting failed. Closed
Application:
Nile Platform
Business Unit:
IT Infrastructure
Sub-Business Unit:
Architecture - Ingestion
Category & SubCategory: Application
IMPACT Description: Customer-facing application or Entire Company affected
URGENCY Description: Outage: Work has stopped and/or there is a large impact on revenue. A same day resolution is required.
Contact Type: Self-Service
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Closed: Tue May 12 03:15:01 EDT 2026
Resolved Time: 11/May/26 03:06 AM EDT
Who else needs to be notified:
Ganapuram, Srivarshini, Padhi, Swayam
Close Notes: We have encountered a spooler failure issue this noon in prod. Based on the observation, the spooler failures were caused due to the NAS spooler relying on a single Axway server endpoint for processing. During the Linux maintenance activity, the server currently being used by Nile was rebooted, which interrupted the spooler processes since there was no load balancing or failover configuration available to route the traffic to another active server. Along with this, authentication failures were also encountered while accessing the Axway transfer log endpoint, which further impacted the spooler flow. Overall, the issue indicates that the current implementation has a dependency on a single server endpoint, making the spooler vulnerable during server maintenance or unexpected outages.

 
In detail:
The spooler currently uses the endpoint https://gad1pt2axcore01:8444/api/v2.0/logs/transfers, and during execution the endpoint encountered an authentication failure while attempting to retrieve file details from the Axway transfer log endpoint before initiating the spooling activity, which caused the spooler flow to fail. Additionally, a Linux patch maintenance activity was being performed during the same timeframe, where the servers were rebooted sequentially, starting with server 01 followed by server 02. Since Nile is currently configured to exclusively use the 01 server for Axway communication, the spooler processes failed when the server went down for maintenance.
A discussion was held with the engineering team regarding the usage of the direct server endpoint instead of the available load-balanced endpoint https://transfer2admin.cotiviti.com. The load balancer endpoint should ideally be used and that similar issues had been observed previously. The engineering team mentioned that they have already decided to migrate the integration to the load-balanced endpoint, and the fix is expected to be included in an upcoming release.
 
The spoolers now seem to be working fine.

 
Elapsed Time: 1 days, 21 hours, 17 minutes, 21.000 seconds
Close code: Solved Permanently
Opened By: Acharya, Suraj
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Estimated Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Groups: None

 Description   

NAS spoolers are being failed.

 Comments

[IT-3044032] Monthly rccv pipeline rccv-healthfirst-powermhs_tba_institutional_claims_processed_GDF2_8_CDF2_8_monthly_new is getting failed for 2026/04 Created: 08/May/26 EDT  Updated: 18/May/26 EDT  Resolved: 18/May/26 EDT

Status: Closed
Project: IT
Affects Version/s: None
Fix Version/s: None

Type: Generic Service Request Priority: Priority 4
Reporter: Kandel, Amik Assignee: Karakavalasa, SriVarshini
Resolution: Resolved Votes: 0
Labels: Monitoring
Remaining Estimate: 0 minutes
Time Spent: 1 hour
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Date Need By: 08/May/26 EDT
GSR Issue Type:
Other
GSR Category:
Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Close Notes: Issue resolved but testing on use cases in dev.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: MICRO < 30 min
Actual Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

Above pipeline is getting failed with below error. It seems like we have processed weekly files. Please crosscheck and help run the pipeline.

Week Number Start Date End Date Data Received Expected Received Filter to be Used
01 20260330 20260405   YES NO  
02 20260406 20260412 20260406_1,20260412_1 YES YES 20260406_1,20260412_1
03 20260413 20260419 20260419_1 YES YES  
04 20260420 20260426   YES NO  
05 20260427 20260503 20260427_1 YES YES  

Summary
Missing data for the following weeks:{}


Week Number Start Date End Date
01 20260330 20260405
04 20260420 20260426

Please notify the escalation contacts about the missing data. If missing data is valid and the error needs to be overridden, set parameter rccv_gdf_institutional_medicalclaims_missing_weekly_data_202604 to 1 in the Client Configuration and proceed with the run.

 Comments   
Comment by Kandel, Amik [ 08/May/26 EDT ]

Confirmed the receipt of the files and proceeding with the ingestion.

Comment by Devatha, Satish [ 08/May/26 EDT ]

pipeline is running now

Comment by Karakavalasa, SriVarshini [ 12/May/26 EDT ]

Hello Amik, 

In the ccv_weekly_data_validation_module, the logic checks for files received within a week specifically for Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday.

 

However, based on the calendar observations:

 

Mar 30th – Apr 5th: File received on Apr 6th
Apr 6th – Apr 12th: Files received on Apr 6th and Apr 12th, both within the same week
Apr 13th – Apr 19th: File received on Apr 19th
Apr 20th – Apr 26th: File received on Apr 27th
Apr 27th – May 3rd: File already received on Apr 27th

 

From the highlighted dates, we can observe that files weren't received within the weekly span. However, during processing, the pipeline continues waiting for an additional file with a date falling within the same week range, which is causing the pipeline failure.

 

To proceed with processing for the April month, we used the override parameter:
rccv_gdf_institutional_medicalclaims_missing_weekly_data_202604
in the client configurations, and the processing has now been completed successfully.

Comment by Karakavalasa, SriVarshini [ 12/May/26 EDT ]

However, i am still working on this module logics to understand deeper and prevent such relevant failures in the future.

Transformed data Impala table independence_ibc_transformed_claim_20260505_1

field statistics
summary

 Comments   
Comment by Suryani, Melly [ 08/May/26 EDT ]

Hi Satish or Sambeg,
Would you please help move this ingestion for Ronnie?
We identified the 6 bad records that caused the truncation here:
 

Ronnie will need to create an ad hoc table, by expaning the column SRC_SYS_CLM_LN_ID from 20 to (maybe) 50 characters.
Ronnie will give you the ad hoc table name, and from there, we need to adjust our pipeline and load it to the ad hoc table.

Thank you so much in advance,

melly

Nile Production Support

 Description   

Hello Nile team
For UHC Cirrus weekly process, EDM is decrypting the files and then copy over for nile to load, the reason is because when we had the action migration to Nile, Nile was not able to decryt the file(I am not sure what was the reason behind it). It has been a long time since we migrate to Nile, would you please check if Nile is able to decryt the files now? So we can plan to stop the EDM file preprocessing and let Nile handle file copying/decrypting/etc.

Thanks and please let me know if more info is needed.

Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

dm-geisinger-facets-weekly_SIU_notouch_file 

has been generating zero parquet output from 2025-07-21 to date. Upon investigation, all records are being rejected due to a delimiter mismatch, as the dataset is using a pipe (|) delimiter instead of a comma (,).

Additionally, there are no validation checks in place within the pipeline to detect or handle such discrepancies.

Could you please review?

 

Pipeline details

Pipeline : dm-geisinger-facets-weekly_SIU_notouch_file

Pipeline ID : 2024-09-04-92280401

Version : V1.2

Env : Prod

 Comments   
Comment by Kantimahanti, Ravisankar [ 12/May/26 EDT ]

waiting for approval from joy

Resolution: Unresolved Votes: 0
Labels: None

Time Spent: Not Specified
Original Estimate: Not Specified

Attachments: HumanaNoTouchCount.xlsx     image.png    
Application:
Decipher - CHC
Business Unit:
Revenue
Sub-Business Unit:
Retrieval Operations
Category & SubCategory: Application
Select the Client Name:
Humana, Inc.
IMPACT Description: Individual Team affected
URGENCY Description: Important: A service is broken or degraded with impact on work or revenue. A resolution is required in one week or less.
Contact Type: Self-Service
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
RCA Client Name: Humana, Inc.
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Who else needs to be notified:
Hafer, Shane, Hawkins, Lisa, Patel, Meghna
Elapsed Time: 19 days, 15 hours, 37 minutes, 59.222 seconds
Opened By: Bianchi, Kristi
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Estimated Size: XSMALL 0 – 1 hr
Groups: None

 Description   

The Humana team is experiencing missing information in the following “No Touch” fields: ReferralSourceCd, NoTouchReason, CategoryCd, SetupDate, NoTouchAmt, and NoTouchFlag. In a few claims that have previous overpayments written in the client’s system (CAS), the fields populate, but the majority are blank.

The team first started seeing a change last week, around April 28th or 29th where the fields were not displaying properly.

The attached snip from CHQT shows a few claim numbers that all have overpayments written in the client system, but most of the “No Touch” fields are blank.

 Comments   
Comment by Bianchi, Kristi [ 06/May/26 EDT ]

Comment by Koehling, James [ 08/May/26 EDT ]

Found HumanaDataArkStaging database on Stage server contained CnlyClaimsNoTouch table containing almost 260 million records.
However, I am unable to locate any of the claims show in the screenshot, though Kristi states there should be a NoTouch flag for them since they have claims written against them.

The are LoadID's for almost every day of the weekday (not weekends) and contain between 20,000 and 60,000 records every day.  I have located a MERGE and DATAMERGE procedures, but nothing which appears to create the new records.  

Checked RAW and SPLIT folders as well as FTP \ B2B \ Humana with 6 folders under that path.  Folder Humana Daily Reports and Inbound were likely candidates, but no recent files in any folder.

Comment by Koehling, James [ 08/May/26 EDT ]

Located several year-old files at \\ccaintranet.com\dfs-fld-01\Audits\Humana\Data Standardization\HumanaNoTouchData

Likely moved to NILE for ingestion.

Issue is that user states client informs that recovery claims have been written on the claims shown in the screenshot.  However only two of the claims are showing a NoTouch flag condition.  No entries found in HumanaDataArkStaging.dbo.CnlyClaimsNoTouch, though the dates appear continuous.  Weekdays appear to be active, weekends are not active.   Need NILE assistance to determine if records have been ingested and present to auditors.

Comment by Koehling, James [ 08/May/26 EDT ]

Counts for weekday NoTouch files.
HumanaNoTouchCount.xlsx

 Description   

Above pipeline is getting failed due to unmatched raw data count.

File name opcsi_provider_incoming_1_20260505.csv
File header record count 646617
File trailer record count 646617
File data line count 646617
Ingested row count 646615
Action Required Investigate and fix if the issue is due to data quality, data load error, or some other reasons. If issue is minor and can be ignored, in Client Configuration set parameter gdf_provider_unmatched_raw_count_override_20260505 to 1 and proceed with the run

Mail subject:Unmatched gdf_provider raw data count - 20260505_1 - prod - dm-optumcare-facets-provider_GDF2_7

Please investigate and help fix the issue.

For monthly optumcare-facets provider pipeline dm-optumcare-facets-provider_GDF2_7 we were unable to ingest a record from the raw client file. There is one record that has an improper line break in the middle of it, causing it to split into two records that are both not able to be ingested.

 

Reached out to CSM to confirm whether or not we can proceed

Nile Production Support
Estimated Size: MICRO < 30 min
Groups: None

 Description   

We have noticed that the timing between when the data load is completed and when that new claim information is available in Datameer is unclear. For example, the data load was completed on Saturday 5/2, however there have not been any new claims loaded into our Datameer reports yet this week. We would like to get a clear and consistent expectation of when the data load information will be loaded into Datameer. A “load date” field in Datameer might also be helpful to better track this. Thank you!

Nile Peer Review: ✅ Yes

Target Environment: NILE PROD

Hi Nile team,

We have 2 files failures for TXCHP that have failed due to being out of sequence meaning it is using a sequence number that has been previously processed by another file.. Can you please ask the client if they meant to send these files as one of the files (txchp_ophistory_incoming_1.05-04-2026_1205) has not loaded into our database which means it should not be giving this kind of error as the sequence number has not been used by another file.

The next available sequence numbers for both these file formats is seq# 794 for professional history files and seq# 795 for institutional history files.

 

SEQ# 451

File Processing Information:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
File                            : txchp_ophistory_incoming_1.05-04-2026_1205

Status: In Progress (HD1)

Labels: Internal_PPM
Remaining Estimate: Not Specified
Time Spent: Not Specified
Original Estimate: Not Specified

GSR Issue Type:

Other
GSR Sub Category:
Other
Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Escalation Point: Joy Del Rosario
Estimated Size: MICRO < 30 min
Internal Assignment Group:
Nile Production Support
Groups: None

 Description   

File                                                         : cigmc_daily_incoming_080050.05-04-2026_0915

QA Status                                            : FAILED

Reason                                                 : FAILED - Excessive failed lines (~56%)

Excessive_fails
A failure due to excessive_fails indicates a file contains errors for over 50% of that file's total number of claim lines. This can be due to duplicate claim line(s) found, which indicates the file's data already exists in the production database. The client may have included this in a previous file sent and did not remove them when sending the new file or wishes for these claims to be reprocessed (which requires a request for the previous file to be wiped prior to sending / loading the new file). This can also be due to invalid data such as with Bad SUBSPEC_ID, which indicates problem with the data in file either being incorrect or not matching the correct format.

 Comments

 Description   

Hello Nile Team,

We have a layout update in file ‘COSMOS_FACL_CONTRACT_CATEGORY_OUT_<>’. Client has added new column ‘FL_MNT_DT’ at the end which is a Date field. We haven’t yet received the latest data dictionary. However, could you please help make updates in the pipeline so that we can ingest the file we received on 04/30.

I have updated the SQL raw staging table ‘UhCosmosDataRaw.dbo.CosmosFaclContractCategory_Holding’ and added field ‘FlMntDT’.

Pipeline Name : prod - dm-uhc-cosmos-actian_migration_cosmosfaclcontractcategory

Thanks,

Priya
